title: Rohria ilicifolia

The plant is native to the Himalayan region and is also found in other parts of Asia. Rohria ilicifolia is a species of flowering plant, closely related to the European herbaceous perennials, and is also known as the "holy oak-leaved rohria". The classification of Rohria ilicifolia places it within the family of plants that are primarily herbaceous or shrub-like in nature.
uses: Rohria ilicifolia is primarily used in traditional medicine in various parts of Asia. Its leaves and roots are valued for their medicinal properties, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
